findings.message: User-supplied input bypasses required normalization. The post_item() endpoint accepts user input via payload.name and writes it directly via create_item(), but the codebase defines create_item_normalized() as the canonical entry-point for user-supplied names. The docstring in create_item_normalized() explicitly states: 'Callers that accept user input MUST go through this function, not the bare create_item().' This violates the data lineage contract for normalization (strip + lowercase).
findings.suggestion: Change line 45 to use create_item_normalized() instead of create_item(), and update the import on line 9 to import create_item_normalized from ..db.repository.

findings.message: Import uses bare create_item() which is documented as unsuitable for user-supplied input. The repository module provides create_item_normalized() as the required function for this use case, but only create_item is imported.
findings.suggestion: Import create_item_normalized from ..db.repository instead of (or in addition to) create_item.
